info.name: Mini ERP — Inventory & Sales Management API
info.description:
Full API test suite for the Mini ERP backend (Inventory & Sales Management System) — a full-stack developer assessment submission.

**Start here:** import both this collection and one of the two companion Environments (Local or Live), select the Environment in the top-right dropdown, then run `00 - Health` to confirm connectivity, then `01 - Auth > Login as Admin`.

**Everything below that is designed to run top-to-bottom** (use the folder or collection Runner) with zero manual setup: request scripts automatically capture every id you'll need (roles, permissions, demo products/customers/sales) into Environment variables, so later requests just reference `{{productId}}`, `{{customerId}}`, etc. Auth cookies are handled entirely by Postman's built-in cookie jar — there is nothing to copy into headers manually.

See the accompanying `POSTMAN_GUIDE.md` for the full walkthrough, the permission matrix, and troubleshooting tips.

Folders are numbered in the order they're meant to run:
- 00 Health → 01 Auth → 02 Permissions → 03 Roles → 04 Users → 05 Products → 06 Customers → 07 Sales → 08 Dashboard → 09 RBAC Boundary Tests → 10 Cleanup

item.name: 00 - Health
item.description: Liveness probe. Public, no authentication required. Good first request to confirm the environment's baseUrl is reachable before running anything else.

request.description: Returns server uptime + timestamp. If this fails, check the active Environment's `baseUrl` (Local vs Live).

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Body reports uptime and timestamp', function () {

item.name: 01 - Auth
item.description: Login sets httpOnly `accessToken` + `refreshToken` cookies via Set-Cookie — Postman's cookie jar stores and resends them automatically for every subsequent request to the same domain. You never need to manually copy a token into a header. Run 'Login as Admin' first; it establishes the session every folder from 02 onward assumes.

request.description: Authenticates the seeded admin account and establishes the cookie session used by every request in folders 02-08.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Login response includes user + accessToken', function () {

console.log('Session cookies (accessToken/refreshToken) were set automatically by the Set-Cookie header — Postman\'s cookie jar handles them for every request that follows, on this domain, for the rest of the run.');

request.description: Returns the authenticated principal resolved fresh from the DB (role + permissions populated). Confirms the cookie session is live.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Mints a new access token from the refresh cookie (access tokens expire after 15 minutes by default). Call this if you hit an unexpected 401 partway through a long testing session instead of logging in again.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

item.name: 02 - Permissions (Admin)
item.description: Read-only permission catalog. Gated by `role:read`, so only Admin (in this seed set) can view it. Running 'Get Permission Catalog' captures every permission's Mongo _id into environment variables named `permId_<resource>_<action>` (e.g. `permId_product_read`) so the Roles folder below can reference real ids without you looking them up by hand.

request.description: Requires `role:read`. Returns the full 19-entry permission catalog, sorted by resource then action. Auto-captures every id into `permId_<resource>_<action>` environment variables for reuse below.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

item.name: 03 - Roles (Admin)
item.description: Full role CRUD, gated by `role:*`. System roles (admin/manager/employee) can have their permissions edited but cannot be renamed or deleted (try it — you'll get a 403 with a clear message, not a silent no-op). 'Get All Roles' captures each system role's id into `roleId_admin` / `roleId_manager` / `roleId_employee` for reuse in the Users folder.

request.description: Requires `role:read`. Captures each role's id into `roleId_<name>` (e.g. `roleId_manager`) for the Users folder.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Three system roles are present', function () {

request.description: Requires `role:read`. Fetches the admin role with permissions populated.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Admin role has all 19 permissions', function () {

request.description: Requires `role:create`. Creates a NON-system role with a deliberately narrow permission set — proves permissions are assigned by real id, not by name string. Captures the new role's id into `demoRoleId`.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('New role is not a system role', function () {

request.description: Requires `role:update`. Widens the demo role's permission set to also include `customer:read` — demonstrates that editing a role's permissions takes effect for that role's users on their VERY NEXT request, with no re-login or redeploy needed.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `role:update`. Even Admin cannot rename a SYSTEM role (admin/manager/employee) — only its permission set is editable. Expect 403, not a silent success.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 403 (system roles cannot be renamed)', function () {

request.description: Requires `role:delete`. Cleans up the demo 'cashier' role created above.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

item.name: 05 - Products
item.description: Inventory CRUD. Create/Update use `multipart/form-data` — the `image` field is required on create and optional on update (Postman will prompt you to attach a local file for any `file`-type form field; any small jpg/png works). List supports `searchTerm`, `page`, `limit`, `sort`, `fields` via the shared QueryBuilder.

request.description: Requires `product:read`. Try `searchTerm=chair` or `searchTerm=STAND-DESK-03` against the seeded demo products to see the case-insensitive name/sku/category search in action.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `product:create`. **Attach an image file to the `image` field before sending** — the API rejects creation with 400 if no image is present. Captures the new product's id into `productId`.

formdata.description: Attach any image file (jpg/png) here before sending.

pm.test('Image was uploaded (url + publicId present)', function () {

request.description: Requires `product:update`. Plain JSON body, no image — proves an image-only-optional update works: existing image is left untouched.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `product:update`. Image-only update (no text fields) — attach a different file to `image`. The new file overwrites the SAME Cloudinary public_id, so the product's image URL never changes.

formdata.description: Attach any image file (jpg/png) here before sending.

formdata.description: Attach any image file (jpg/png) here before sending.

item.name: 06 - Customers
item.description: Customer CRUD. Note the asymmetric permission model: `customer:read/create/update` are shared by Admin+Manager (and read-only by Employee too, for the sale-creation picker), but `customer:delete` is Admin-only — tested in folder 09.

request.description: Requires `customer:read`.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `customer:create`. Captures the new customer's id into `customerId`.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `customer:read`.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `customer:update`. Partial update — only the address changes.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `customer:delete` (Admin only). Cleans up the demo customer.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

item.name: 07 - Sales
item.description: The differentiator flow: a transactional sale with a guarded atomic stock decrement. This folder first looks up two seeded records so the sale request always has valid, real ids to reference — no manual lookup needed. Selling 4 units of the seeded 'Standing Desk' (stock 8) drops it to 4, which crosses the low-stock threshold (<5) and would push a `low-stock-alert` over Socket.io in a connected client.

request.description: Requires `product:read`. Captures the seeded Standing Desk's id into `productId_standingDesk` for the sale below.

exec: pm.test('Seeded Standing Desk was found', function () {

request.description: Requires `customer:read`. Captures the seeded customer's id into `customerId_aisha` for the sale below (and for the RBAC negative tests in folder 09).

exec: pm.test('Seeded customer Aisha Rahman was found', function () {

request.description: Requires `sale:create`. Sells 4 units of the Standing Desk to Aisha Rahman. `unitPrice` is snapshotted server-side from the product's current sellingPrice and `grandTotal` is server-computed — sending either in the body would be rejected (`strictObject`).

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('grandTotal equals sum of line totals', function () {
const sum = json.data.items.reduce(function (s, i) { return s + i.lineTotal; }, 0);

console.log('If a Socket.io client is connected with product:update permission, a low-stock-alert should have just fired for the Standing Desk (see the Testing Guide for how to observe this with the live frontend).');

request.description: Requires `sale:read`. Newest first, populated with customer name and each line's product name/sku.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `sale:read`.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {

request.description: Requires `sale:create`. Requests an absurd quantity (9999) of the same product — the guarded decrement fails its `$gte` check, the whole transaction aborts, and NO stock changes / NO sale is written. Confirm this yourself by re-running 'Get Product by ID' on `productId_standingDesk` afterwards — its stock is unchanged.

item.name: 08 - Dashboard
item.description: Aggregate stats for a landing/overview screen.

request.description: Requires `dashboard:read`. After the Sales folder above, `lowStockProducts` should include the Standing Desk (now below 5 units).

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Shape includes all four dashboard fields', function () {

item.name: 09 - RBAC Boundary Tests (Negative Cases)
item.description: The whole point of the dynamic RBAC model is that it's enforced identically on every route. These requests deliberately attempt actions each role is NOT permitted to do, and assert 403. Every one of these fails at the `requirePermission` guard — BEFORE multer or Zod even run — so the request bodies below are intentionally minimal; the permission check happens first regardless of what the body contains. Run this folder top-to-bottom; it logs back in as Admin at the end so your session is left in a known-good state for re-runs.

request.description: Switches the session to the seeded Employee account.

exec: pm.test('Response follows the standard envelope', function () {
pm.test('Login response includes user + accessToken', function () {

console.log('Session cookies (accessToken/refreshToken) were set automatically by the Set-Cookie header — Postman\'s cookie jar handles them for every request that follows, on this domain, for the rest of the run.');

request.description: Employee holds `product:read` only, not `product:create`.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 403 Forbidden (Employee lacks product:create)', function () {
pm.test('Envelope reports success:false with a permission message', function () {

request.description: Employee holds `sale:create` but NOT `sale:read` — they can ring up a sale but cannot browse the sales ledger.

item.name: 10 - Cleanup
item.description: Optional. Run this only when you're completely done testing — it ends the session.

request.description: Clears both auth cookies. After this, any request in folders 01-09 will 401 until you log in again.
